reviews.review_title: Critical bug CANT CONNECT Google Drive
reviews.review_description:
Amazing app with so much potential, really clean UI and page turning with hand tracking feels good!

But I just CAN'T USE THE APP - clicking on Google Drive to connect CRASHES the app.

Please fix this! 🙏🏼🥺

reviews.review_title: Needs an update but it works!!!!
reviews.review_description:
It's amazing to read comics here, I would happily pay for an updated version!

I really enjoy this app.

reviews.review_title: A New, Enjoyable way to read Old Comics
reviews.review_description:
Finally!  An app that allows reading of pdf's and cbz's that is incredibly easy on the eyes.  The size at which you can increase/decrease the size of the pdf/cbz is wonderful as it allows various types and sizes of fonts to be properly sized for easy reading.

It took me a couple of tries to get the pdf's/cbz's to show up in the app library through the Quest itself, but I'll attribute that to operator error.

I agree with other commentors on the desire to have more formats available (e.g., cbr). If using the Quest as the storage space, I recommend using those trade paperback collections, compared to individual issues.  If you have WinRAR, converting from cbr to cbz is doable, but would prefer cbr to be natively supported.
However, I think there is a size limitation the app can read.  I tried a cbz of 2.2GB and the app wouldn't display it, but I can confirm a cbz size of 1.2GB is readable.

Lastly, some of the artwork in a cbz can have a 'shimmering' feel to it, which oddly, for myself, enhances the experience.

Overall, this app is well worth the effort, and I would pay to see improvements to this app.

reviews.review_title: Add smb, epub support and I’ll try again
reviews.review_description:
I don’t use google drive and I can’t be bothered to copy individual files onto my device. 

I have a large collection e-books, comics and magazines on my local NAS that I’d love to be able to browse in my headset. If this app allowed browsing an smb share and loading epub formatted books, it would become interesting. For now, it offers nothing I want, but maybe has the most potential of the few reader apps I have tried so far.

This app is great and unique. 

It has an amazing potential and I think it would be a mistake for the dev to stop at this point. 

To the dev: I contacted you on Reddit, I hope you'll see my message and get back to me! Congrats on what you've achieved so far.

This is a worth while app, finally a good use to vr is being given. Understanding that it's still under developement, I have to say... it's not perfect, hand controls are still sketchy and other minor details... But there is one thing that is really stupid, because it's missing.... a search option!!!!! The possibility to search for a string of text! Please consider to add it in the future if it's still in fact being developed.

In general, good job, good app, I use it a lot.

The app's purpose is pretty straigt-forward: reading books in vr. If you take that into account, the app is succesful. Nevertheless, it could use with some updates regarding the enviroment such as maybe color or different places. My favorite feature is by far the sound-enviroment update. I don't mind the bugs, I get that this is applab, so I'm definetly keen on seeing where this app ends up

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your understanding, simple straightforward reading in VR is exactly what I set out to achieve with Livro.
I'd love to be able to update the app with extra features in the future :)

reviews.review_description: I publish a guide to VR in pdf format … and now you can read it in VR! I uploaded it to the app via sidequest and it works great. I like that you can supersize it with the thumbstick. The text reads easily, very clear and comfortable. Page flipping takes a bit of getting used to but it works. This will be great with Q3 colour passthrough too. Clever name (Livro is Portuguese for book). Nice job!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, glad to hear you enjoyed the app :)

reviews.review_description:
I wasnt actually looking for a comic book reader when I bought this but the prospect of hand tracking and google drive integration made this too good to pass up. Testing it out with a single comic it took some getting used to but I was comfortably navigating the interface with handtracking within 10 minutes. A feature I really liked is that you can resize the comics. I did find that the test comic I used was a bit fuzzy at a regular size but once stretched out to newspaper size it was a smooth read. 
I give it 4 out of 5 because I would love to see CBR support.

developer_response.body: Hey! Thanks for the review, I'd love to get CBR support in the future!

reviews.review_title: This is what quest was missing
reviews.review_description: Amazing app now i can finally put in a drawer a kindle.please keep doing updated as there are no apps like this.
Very great app. I wish if you profide edting just like highliting etc

reviews.review_title: Close, but still some way to go...
reviews.review_description:
I'm enjoying the potential of this app but you can tell it's still very much in app lab development and needs just a few rough edges sorting out: finding out where the app actually stores books in the quest file structure is non-existent, meaning you're not able to load books in via browser on a PC; downloading from Google drive doesn't work at the moment, not being able to get past the login screen; opening books from successfully downloading via the quest in-house browser is a recognized issue, requiring a unit lock to restart the book rendering... It's just too glitchy right now.

There are big pros, however: gorgeous rendering of the books when they do work, a great soothing reading environment and it's a cracking stab at XR and hand control which you really see what can be achieved. I can't wait for the app to get the full development attention it needs to fully bring it up to speed. For everyone else, though, you have to be aware - if you're looking for a solid comics reader in Livro, it's not quite there yet.
Il would be great to make a beautiful library and to be able to rename the books.

I hope a new update comes soon, I feel like the pages appear squashed and after a while passing pages there is certain point the images are not loading correctly.
Also would be nice if we can upload our own audio, that way we can upload audiobooks, I think 1 button to play and pause would be cool.
Sorry I can not give you good evaluation, but please keep going, it is a good start.

developer_response.body: Hey! Thanks for giving the app a try and for the suggestions. Sorry for the inconvenience, hopefully we can resolve these issues soon.

reviews.review_title: I'm still trying to figure it out
reviews.review_description: How do we add books

developer_response.body: Hey! Have a look in the UserGuide that comes pre-loaded with the app. You can choose between loading files from internal storage or Google drive.

reviews.review_description: The connection to Google Drive doesn’t work, thereby making this app purchase frivolous. It would be rad if the app for reading books had the functionality to let you read your books.

Works great with a minor bug. The ability to read pdfs offline is something I'm surprised the Quest 2 isn't capable of already. Formatting files into book form is a plus. Only thing I would like to see is a completely dark (black) environment to block everything out when I'm reading. That should be easy to add. I'm not really a fan of the default environment, but passthrough is enough.

As I see multiple people have the same issue, connecting to google drive won't work... it redirects to the login/permission page in the browser, but once I proceed it brings me ba kto the app and the connection to google drive isn't working... this is unfortunately a deal breaker as I want to have my book library in one central location rather than downloading it on every one of my devices...

Other than that the app looks great! Maybe a bit of tweaking with the UI/usability but very good nonetheless. Hope this gets fixed I I won't be using this until it works properly...

reviews.review_title: Perfect app for reading books in VR
reviews.review_description: While I would like the app to have support for other formats (.epub, .mobi, .awz), it does what's intended to do and it's amazing at it. Can't recommend it enough!

reviews.review_title: Good but could be better with updates
reviews.review_description: I really enjoy using this app but the big issue I have is I couldn't connect my Google drive account other than that this is a good app
